name: Standing desks
summary: Motorized height-adjustable standing desks for home and office use.

evidence.claim: BIFMA X5.5 is the industry standard for standing desk stability, measured by deflection at standing height under specified loads. Few manufacturers publish BIFMA test results.

criteria.name: height_range_inches
criteria.notes: Lower minimum is better for shorter users (<5'4''); higher max is better for tall users (>6'2'').

criteria.name: stability_score
criteria.notes: Sway at standing height under load. BIFMA X5.5 is the rigorous standard (E1).

confabulationPatterns.reality: Not a defined term. BIFMA X5.5 testing is rigorous; few consumer desks actually pass (E1). Look for cited BIFMA or third-party stability data, not marketing.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is BIFMA X5.5 certification or third-party stability testing cited?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Maximum capacity figures assume centered static load; dynamic stability at standing height is a different test. A desk rated to 300 lbs may wobble noticeably at the 30" extended height under a 150 lb actual load.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the load rating static or dynamic? Does it match stability at extended height?

compatibilityQuestions: Height: below 5'4''? Ensure low-end range is <25 inches.
Planning to add monitor arms? Weight capacity and desktop area matter.
Hard floor vs carpet? Stability perception changes.

repairability.notes: Uplift and Fully have strong parts support. Budget brands have limited parts.
